source: classic real-world Go pitfall (defer inside a loop)

expected_bug.summary: defer f.Close() inside the for loop does NOT run per iteration — deferred calls run when the enclosing FUNCTION returns. So every opened file stays open until processFiles returns; on a large batch this accumulates thousands of open descriptors and exhausts the fd limit (too many open files).
expected_bug.l_code_hint: L8 (resource — descriptor not released on the expected path)

fixed_change: Extract processOne(p): the defer now runs at each call's return, so at most one descriptor is open at a time.
